mission.title: Install and Configure kubeadm (Kubernetes bootstrapper)
mission.description: Kubeadm is a tool designed to simplify the process of creating and managing Kubernetes clusters. It provides best-practice 'fast paths' for bootstrapping clusters, making it suitable for both development and production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for manual configuration of certain components and a reliance on the underlying infrastructure for high availability.

steps.title: Install Kubeadm
steps.description:
This step installs the kubeadm binary on your system, which is necessary for initializing and managing your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -s https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | sudo apt-key add -

echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list

sudo apt-get update

sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubeadm kubectl

```

> Add the Google Cloud public key for package verification.
> Add the Kubernetes APT repository.
> Update the package list to include the Kubernetes packages.
> Install specific versions of kubelet, kubeadm, and kubectl.
> Prevent the packages from being automatically updated.
commands: echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00
sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubeadm kubectl

steps.title: Initialize the Kubernetes Control Plane
steps.description:
This step initializes the Kubernetes control plane on the master node, setting up the necessary components.

```bash

sudo kubeadm init --kubernetes-version=v1.31.0 --pod-network-cidr=192.168.0.0/16

mkdir -p $HOME/.kube

sudo cp -i /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf $HOME/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) $HOME/.kube/config

```

> Initialize the cluster with a specific Kubernetes version and pod network CIDR.
> Create a directory for kubeconfig.
> Copy the admin configuration file to the user's kubeconfig.
> Change ownership of the kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Install a Pod Network Add-on
steps.description:
This step installs a network add-on to enable communication between pods in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://docs.projectcalico.org/manifests/calico.yaml

```

> Install Calico as the network add-on.

steps.title: Verify the Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their status.

uninstall.title: Reset the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes all Kubernetes components and configurations from the host. Warning: This will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

sudo kubeadm reset

sudo rm -rf $HOME/.kube

```

> Reset the kubeadm configuration.
> Remove the kubeconfig directory.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster state, drain the node, upgrade the components, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

sudo kubeadm upgrade plan

sudo kubeadm upgrade apply v1.31.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the status of nodes before upgrading.
> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Check available upgrades.
> Upgrade the control plane.
> Uncordon the node after upgrade.
> Verify that the nodes are in 'Ready' state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubelet service may not be started or is failing to run.

**Fix:**
Run 'sudo systemctl start kubelet' to start the kubelet service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeadm init failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to insufficient resources or a misconfigured environment.

**Fix:**
Ensure your system meets the minimum requirements and check the logs with 'journalctl -xeu kubelet'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate is valid for ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a certificate issue with the API server.

**Fix:**
Regenerate the certificates using 'kubeadm certs renew all'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'node not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the node has not joined the cluster properly.

**Fix:**
Re-run 'kubeadm join' with the correct token and discovery token CA cert hash.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for the new PodSecurity admission controller, improved handling of kubelet configuration.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new PodSecurity policies.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' state.

sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00
sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubeadm kubectl

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the resource requirements and has the necessary tools installed.
